# Cloudinary Test URLs

Since we're using Cloudinary's Fetch API, here are some example URLs that will be automatically transformed:

## Example S3 URLs and their Cloudinary equivalents:

### Example 1: AI Generated Image
**Original S3 URL:**
```
https://mydscvr-event-images.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/ai-images/event123.jpg
```

**Cloudinary Mobile URL (auto-generated by the app):**
```
https://res.cloudinary.com/dikjgzjsq/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto,w_800/https%3A%2F%2Fmydscvr-event-images.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fai-images%2Fevent123.jpg
```

### Example 2: Event Image
**Original S3 URL:**
```
https://mydscvr-event-images.s3.amazonaws.com/events/dubai-shopping-festival.jpg
```

**Cloudinary Mobile URL:**
```
https://res.cloudinary.com/dikjgzjsq/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto,w_800/https%3A%2F%2Fmydscvr-event-images.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fevents%2Fdubai-shopping-festival.jpg
```

## How it works:

1. The app detects S3 URLs automatically
2. For mobile browsers, it converts them to Cloudinary URLs with:
   - `f_auto` - Automatic format (WebP for Chrome, JPEG for Safari)
   - `q_auto` - Automatic quality based on image content
   - `w_800` - Maximum width of 800px for mobile

3. Cloudinary fetches the image from S3 on first request and caches it

## Testing:

1. Open the app on your mobile device
2. Open Developer Tools (if available) or check network requests
3. Look for image URLs - they should all start with `https://res.cloudinary.com/dikjgzjsq/`

## Benefits:

- **No CORS issues** - Cloudinary handles cross-origin requests
- **Automatic optimization** - Images are compressed and converted to optimal formats
- **CDN delivery** - Images are served from Cloudinary's global CDN
- **No encoding errors** - All processing happens server-side

## Manual Test:

You can test any S3 image URL by encoding it and appending to:
```
https://res.cloudinary.com/dikjgzjsq/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto,w_800/[ENCODED_S3_URL]
```

For example, to test with any event image you find in the app, just check the network tab for the Cloudinary URL being used.
